metadata.description: Awesome Claude Code plugins — a curated list of slash commands, subagents, MCP servers, and hooks for Claude Code

plugins.name: documentation-generator
plugins.description: Create comprehensive documentation for code, APIs, and projects.

plugins.name: lyra
plugins.description: Lyra - a master-level AI prompt optimization specialist.

plugins.name: analyze-codebase
plugins.description: Generate comprehensive analysis and documentation of entire codebase

plugins.name: update-claudemd
plugins.description: Automatically update CLAUDE.md file based on recent code changes

plugins.name: ultrathink
plugins.description: Use /ultrathink <TASK_DESCRIPTION> to launch a Coordinator Agent that directs four specialist sub-agents—Architect, Research, Coder, and Tester—to analyze, design, implement, and validate your coding task. The process breaks the task into clear steps, gathers insights, and synthesizes a cohesive solution with actionable outputs. Relevant files can be referenced ad-hoc using @ filename syntax.

plugins.name: code-review
plugins.description: Perform a comprehensive code review of recent changes

plugins.name: refractor
plugins.description: Refactor code following best practices and design patterns

plugins.name: code-review-assistant
plugins.description: Get comprehensive code reviews with suggestions for improvements, best practices, and potential issues.

plugins.name: bug-detective
plugins.description: Systematically debug issues with step-by-step troubleshooting approaches.

plugins.name: audit
plugins.description: Perform security audit on codebase

plugins.name: plan
plugins.description: For easy problems, start here. For harder problems, do this after Explore.

plugins.name: claude-desktop-extension
plugins.description: This command provides the context necessary for Claude Code to create the Desktop Extension or .dxt file of an MCP.

plugins.name: optimize
plugins.description: Analyze and optimize code performance

plugins.name: commit
plugins.description: Creates git commits using conventional commit format with appropriate emojis, following project standards and creating descriptive messages that explain the purpose of changes.

plugins.name: explore
plugins.description:
Helps Claude read a planning document and explore related files to get familiar with a topic. Asking Claude to prepare to discuss seems to work better than asking it to prepare to do specific work.

This is followed by Plan, then Execute.

plugins.name: generate-api-docs
plugins.description: Generate API documentation for endpoints

plugins.name: debug-session
plugins.description: Ask Claude Code to help you debug an issue

plugins.name: test-file
plugins.description: Generate comprehensive tests for a specific file

plugins.name: double-check
plugins.description: An easy way to force agent to think again if it's statement that the "Job is done and production ready" is actually done - usually it's not. Thanks to this command you don't have to check after the agent if they did their job.

plugins.name: create-worktrees
plugins.description: Creates git worktrees for all open PRs or specific branches, handling branches with slashes, cleaning up stale worktrees, and supporting custom branch creation for development.

plugins.name: pr-review
plugins.description: Reviews pull request changes to provide feedback, check for issues, and suggest improvements before merging into the main codebase.

plugins.name: update-branch-name
plugins.description: Updates branch names with proper prefixes and formats, enforcing naming conventions, supporting semantic prefixes, and managing remote branch updates.

plugins.name: analyze-issue
plugins.description: Fetches GitHub issue details to create comprehensive implementation specifications, analyzing requirements and planning structured approach with clear implementation steps.

plugins.name: discuss
plugins.description: Collaborative technical discussion with proactive requirements gathering

plugins.name: bug-fix
plugins.description: Streamlines bug fixing by creating a GitHub issue first, then a feature branch for implementing and thoroughly testing the solution before merging.

plugins.name: openapi-expert
plugins.description: Use this agent to update, synchronize, or validate the OpenAPI specification (openapi.yml) against the actual REST API implementation. This includes adding new endpoints, updating request/response schemas, fixing discrepancies between the spec and code, or ensuring complete API documentation coverage.

plugins.name: create-pr
plugins.description: Streamlines pull request creation by handling the entire workflow: creating a new branch, committing changes, formatting modified files with Biome, and submitting the PR.

plugins.name: create-pull-request
plugins.description: Provides comprehensive PR creation guidance with GitHub CLI, enforcing title conventions, following template structure, and offering concrete command examples with best practices.

plugins.name: fix-github-issue
plugins.description: Analyzes and fixes GitHub issues using a structured approach with GitHub CLI for issue details, implementing necessary code changes, running tests, and creating proper commit messages.

plugins.name: fix-issue
plugins.description: Addresses GitHub issues by taking issue number as parameter, analyzing context, implementing solution, and testing/validating the fix for proper integration.

plugins.name: fix-pr
plugins.description: Fetches and fixes unresolved PR comments by automatically retrieving feedback, addressing reviewer concerns, making targeted code improvements, and streamlining the review process.

plugins.name: husky
plugins.description: Sets up and manages Husky Git hooks by configuring pre-commit hooks, establishing commit message standards, integrating with linting tools, and ensuring code quality on commits.

plugins.name: 2-commit-fast
plugins.description: Automates git commit process by selecting the first suggested message, generating structured commits with consistent formatting while skipping manual confirmation and removing Claude co-Contributorship footer

plugins.name: pr-issue-resolve
plugins.description: this is to analyze the PRs and solve the requested changes in them

plugins.name: github-issue-fix
plugins.description: This is a detailed way you can analyze the GitHub issues and let Claude handle them in best possible way.

plugins.name: accessibility-expert
plugins.description: Examples:

plugins.name: ai-engineer
plugins.description: Use this agent to implement AI/ML features such as LLMs, recommenders, and visual intelligence for fast deployment. Examples: <example> Context: Recommendations user: "Need AI content recommendations" assistant: "Using ai-engineer to build a behavior‑based recommendation pipeline." <commentary> Recommenders need solid ML logic and continuous learning. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: LLM chatbot user: "Add an AI chatbot" assistant: "Using ai-engineer to integrate an LLM with prompts, streaming, and safe response handling." <commentary> LLM features require prompt design and token/latency management. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: Visual search user: "Search products from a photo" assistant: "Using ai-engineer to add image recognition and similarity matching." <commentary> CV systems require efficient inference and proper model choice. </commentary> </example>

plugins.name: ai-ethics-governance-specialist
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to implement AI ethics frameworks, governance policies, and responsible AI practices for B2B applications. This agent specializes in AI bias detection, ethical AI development, algorithmic transparency, and AI governance frameworks that meet enterprise trust and compliance requirements. Examples:

plugins.name: analytics-reporter
plugins.description:
Use this agent when analyzing metrics, generating insights from data, creating performance reports, or making data-driven recommendations. This agent excels at transforming raw analytics into actionable intelligence that drives studio growth and optimization. Examples:

<example>
Context: Monthly performance review needed

plugins.name: angelos-symbo
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to create or convert prompts using the SYMBO (symbolic) notation system. This agent MUST be activated whenever generating SYMBO prompts or converting existing prompts to symbolic format. It ensures proper symbolic notation and rule compliance for task management systems, code review analyzers, and any other prompt engineering needs. Invoke it when a user requests a new SYMBO prompt, wants to convert natural language prompts to SYMBO notation, or needs symbolic prompt optimization.

plugins.name: api-integration-specialist
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to design and implement internal API architecture, developer experience, and API infrastructure for B2B applications. This agent specializes in REST API design, GraphQL implementation, API documentation, SDK development, and developer portal creation. Handles API performance optimization, versioning strategies, and internal service communication. Examples:

plugins.name: api-tester
plugins.description:
Use this agent for comprehensive API testing including performance testing, load testing, and contract testing. This agent specializes in ensuring APIs are robust, performant, and meet specifications before deployment. Examples:

<example>
Context: Testing API performance under load

plugins.name: app-store-optimizer
plugins.description:
Use this agent when preparing app store listings, researching keywords, optimizing app metadata, improving conversion rates, or analyzing app store performance. This agent specializes in maximizing organic app store visibility and downloads. Examples:

<example>
Context: Preparing for app launch

plugins.name: b2b-project-shipper
plugins.description: PROACTIVELY use this agent when approaching B2B launch milestones, enterprise release deadlines, or B2B go-to-market activities. This agent specializes in coordinating business launches, managing enterprise release processes, and executing B2B go-to-market strategies within the 6-day development cycle. Should be triggered automatically when enterprise release dates are set, B2B launch plans are needed, or business market positioning is discussed. Examples:

plugins.name: backend-architect
plugins.description: Use this agent when designing APIs, building server-side logic, implementing databases, or architecting scalable backend systems. It focuses on secure, performant backend services including REST API design with authentication and rate limiting, database query optimization and indexing strategies, and OAuth2/authentication system implementation. Ideal for balancing security, scalability, and maintainability in server-side architecture.

plugins.name: brand-guardian
plugins.description:
Use this agent when establishing brand guidelines, ensuring visual consistency, managing brand assets, or evolving brand identity. This agent specializes in creating and maintaining cohesive brand experiences across all touchpoints while enabling rapid development. Examples:

<example>
Context: Creating brand guidelines for a new app

plugins.name: ceo-quality-controller-agent
plugins.description: Universal quality‑control orchestrator and final authority for software projects. Discovers and coordinates sub‑agents, performs comprehensive quality checks, security validation, and deployment‑readiness reviews, adapting to any project, language, or framework while enforcing enterprise‑grade standards. Examples: <example>Context: Code ready for review user: 'Please review this code before commit' assistant: 'I'll use the 1‑ceo‑quality‑control‑agent to orchestrate full quality validation, engage specialists, and run final security checks before approval.' <commentary>Universal quality control ensures consistent validation across projects.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Multi‑agent work completed user: 'Several agents finished tasks, need quality review' assistant: 'Engaging the 1‑ceo‑quality‑control‑agent to coordinate validation across all outputs and confirm standards.' <commentary>Centralized authority ensures coherent multi‑agent quality.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: changelog-generator
plugins.description: Changelog Generator subagent

plugins.name: code-architect
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to design scalable architecture and folder structures for new features or projects. Examples include: when starting a new feature module, refactoring existing code organization, planning microservice boundaries, designing component hierarchies, or establishing project structure conventions. For example: user: 'I need to add a user authentication system to my app' -> assistant: 'I'll use the code-architect agent to design the architecture and folder structure for your authentication system' -> <uses agent>. Another example: user: 'How should I organize my e-commerce product catalog feature?' -> assistant: 'Let me use the code-architect agent to design a scalable structure for your product catalog' -> <uses agent>.

plugins.name: code-reviewer
plugins.description: Expert code review specialist. Proactively reviews code for quality, security, and maintainability. Use immediately after writing or modifying code.

plugins.name: codebase-documenter
plugins.description: Use this agent to analyze a service or codebase component and generate up‑to‑date CLAUDE.md documentation. Invoke it after adding new services, major refactors, or whenever docs must reflect the current codebase. Examples: <example>Context: New authentication service needs documentation user: 'I finished the auth service, can you document it?' assistant: 'Using the codebase-documenter agent to analyze the auth service and create detailed CLAUDE.md documentation.' <commentary>Use the codebase-documenter agent to produce complete CLAUDE.md files for finished services.</commentary></example> <example>Context: New API module requires team documentation user: 'We need docs for the new payment API.' assistant: 'Using the codebase-documenter agent to document the payment processing API.' <commentary>Use this agent to generate CLAUDE.md files with setup details and architectural notes for new APIs.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: compliance-automation-specialist
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to automate compliance processes for SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, and other enterprise regulatory requirements. This agent specializes in compliance automation, audit preparation, continuous monitoring, and regulatory framework implementation for B2B platforms. Examples:

plugins.name: content-creator
plugins.description: Content Creator subagent

plugins.name: context7-docs-fetcher
plugins.description:
Use this agent to fetch and use documentation from Context7 for specific libraries or frameworks. Examples: <example> Context: User building a React app needs docs for hooks. user: "I need to use useState and useEffect"
assistant: "Using the context7-docs-fetcher agent to retrieve the latest React hooks documentation." <commentary> Use the context7-docs-fetcher agent to pull accurate, up-to-date React docs. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: User working with Express.js and MongoDB needs setup guidance. user: "How do I build a REST API with Express and MongoDB?"
assistant: "Using the context7-docs-fetcher agent to fetch current Express.js and MongoDB documentation." <commentary> When multiple libraries are involved, use the context7-docs-fetcher agent to retrieve comprehensive docs. </commentary> </example>

plugins.name: customer-success-manager
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to optimize customer success operations for B2B enterprise clients. This agent specializes in customer health monitoring, expansion revenue identification, churn prevention, enterprise account management, and customer lifecycle optimization. Handles enterprise onboarding, adoption tracking, and strategic account growth. Examples:

plugins.name: data-privacy-engineer
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to implement data privacy engineering, GDPR compliance, data protection frameworks, and privacy-by-design principles for B2B applications. This agent specializes in privacy engineering, data minimization, consent management, and global privacy regulation compliance for enterprise platforms. Examples:

plugins.name: data-scientist
plugins.description: Data analysis expert for SQL queries, BigQuery operations, and data insights. Use proactively for data analysis tasks and queries.

plugins.name: database-performance-optimizer
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to optimize database performance for B2B applications at enterprise scale. This agent specializes in multi-tenant database optimization, query performance tuning, indexing strategies, connection pooling, and database scaling for SaaS platforms. Handles PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, and cloud database optimizations. Examples:

plugins.name: debugger
plugins.description: Debugging specialist for errors, test failures, and unexpected behavior. Use proactively when encountering any issues.

plugins.name: deployment-engineer
plugins.description:
Use this agent when setting up CI/CD pipelines, configuring Docker containers, deploying applications to cloud platforms, setting up Kubernetes clusters, implementing infrastructure as code, or automating deployment workflows. Examples:

<example>Context: User is setting up a new project and needs deployment automation.
user: "I've built a FastAPI application and need to deploy it to production with proper CI/CD"
assistant: "I'll use the deployment-engineer agent to set up a complete deployment pipeline with Docker, GitHub Actions, and production-ready configurations." </example>

<example>Context: User mentions containerization or deployment issues.
user: "Our deployment process is manual and error-prone. We need to automate it."
assistant: "Let me use the deployment-engineer agent to design an automated CI/CD pipeline that eliminates manual steps and ensures reliable deployments." </example>

plugins.name: desktop-app-dev
plugins.description: Desktop App Dev subagent

plugins.name: devops-automator
plugins.description: Use this agent when setting up CI/CD pipelines, configuring cloud infrastructure, implementing monitoring, or automating deployments. It focuses on reliable, fast deployment and operations. Handles automated deployment pipelines with testing and build gates, infrastructure auto-scaling and load balancing for traffic resilience, and monitoring and alerting setup for fast detection and recovery of production issues.

plugins.name: enterprise-integrator-architect
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to design and implement complex external enterprise system integrations for B2B applications. This agent specializes in connecting your platform with Salesforce, HubSpot, Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, SAP, Oracle ERP, and other critical third-party business software. Handles external API orchestration, data synchronization with enterprise systems, webhook management for third-party services, and enterprise-grade integration patterns. Examples:

plugins.name: enterprise-onboarding-specialist
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to design and optimize complex enterprise customer onboarding processes involving multiple stakeholders, change management, and organizational adoption. This agent specializes in enterprise implementation strategy, stakeholder management, change management, and large-scale rollout coordination for B2B platforms. Examples:

plugins.name: enterprise-security-reviewer
plugins.description: Use this agent for comprehensive B2B security assessments, enterprise compliance validation, multi-tenant security reviews, and security audit preparation. This agent specializes in SOC 2, GDPR, ISO 27001 compliance and enterprise-grade security implementations for B2B SaaS platforms. Examples:

plugins.name: experiment-tracker
plugins.description: PROACTIVELY use this agent when experiments are created, modified, or need analysis. It tracks A/B tests, feature experiments, and improvements in the 6-day cycle, triggering when feature flags or experimental paths appear. Covers feature flag setup and documentation, live experiment monitoring and early result recording, weekly milestone compilation, and data-driven product decisions on whether to keep, modify, or remove features.

plugins.name: feedback-synthesizer
plugins.description:
Use this agent when you need to analyze user feedback from multiple sources, identify patterns in user complaints or requests, synthesize insights from reviews, or prioritize feature development based on user input. This agent excels at turning raw feedback into actionable product insights. Examples:

<example>
Context: Weekly review of user feedback

plugins.name: finance-tracker
plugins.description:
Use this agent when managing budgets, optimizing costs, forecasting revenue, or analyzing financial performance. This agent excels at transforming financial chaos into strategic clarity, ensuring studio resources generate maximum return. Examples:

<example>
Context: Planning next quarter's development budget

plugins.name: flutter-mobile-app-dev
plugins.description: Use this agent for expert Flutter mobile development help, including code analysis, widget creation, debugging, performance tuning, and architectural decisions. Examples: <example> Context: Navigation issue in a Flutter app user: 'My Navigator.push isn't updating the UI correctly when moving to a new screen' assistant: 'I'll use the flutter-dev agent to review your navigation setup and fix the issue.' <commentary> Flutter-specific navigation bugs should use the flutter-dev agent.</commentary></example> <example> Context: Creating a custom widget to match a design system user: 'I need to create a custom button widget that matches our app's design system' assistant: 'I'll use the flutter-dev agent to build a button widget that fits your codebase and patterns.' <commentary> The flutter-dev agent ensures widgets follow existing app conventions.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: frontend-developer
plugins.description: Use this agent when building user interfaces, implementing React/Vue/Angular components, managing state, or optimizing frontend performance. It creates responsive, accessible, high-performance web apps. Handles dashboard and data-rich UI creation, responsive design fixes for cross-device compatibility, and frontend performance optimization including virtualization and efficient rendering for large datasets.

plugins.name: growth-hacker
plugins.description: Growth Hacker subagent

plugins.name: infrastructure-maintainer
plugins.description:
Use this agent when monitoring system health, optimizing performance, managing scaling, or ensuring infrastructure reliability. This agent excels at keeping studio applications running smoothly while preparing for growth and preventing disasters. Examples:

<example>
Context: App experiencing slow performance

plugins.name: instagram-curator
plugins.description: Instagram Curator subagent

plugins.name: joker
plugins.description:
Use this agent when you need to lighten the mood, create funny content, or add humor to any situation. This agent specializes in dad jokes, programming puns, and startup humor. Examples:

<example>
Context: Team needs a laugh during a stressful sprint
user: "We've been debugging for hours and everyone's frustrated"
assistant: "Time for a morale boost! Let me use the joker agent to share some programming humor."
<commentary>
Humor can help reset team energy during challenging moments.
</commentary>
</example>

<example>
Context: Creating fun error messages
user: "Our 404 page is boring"
assistant: "Let's make that error page memorable! I'll use the joker agent to create some funny 404 messages."
<commentary>
Humorous error pages can turn frustration into delight.
</commentary>
</example>

plugins.name: legal-advisor
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need legal advisory, compliance documentation, RFP response creation, and enterprise contract support for B2B applications. This agent specializes in legal document creation, regulatory analysis, enterprise procurement compliance, and RFP winning strategies. Transforms complex legal requirements into competitive advantages. Examples:

plugins.name: legal-compliance-checker
plugins.description:
Use this agent when reviewing terms of service, privacy policies, ensuring regulatory compliance, or handling legal requirements. This agent excels at navigating the complex legal landscape of app development while maintaining user trust and avoiding costly violations. Examples:

<example>
Context: Launching app in European markets

plugins.name: mobile-app-builder
plugins.description: Use this agent for native iOS/Android apps, React Native features, and mobile performance tuning. It specializes in smooth, native-feeling experiences. Examples: <example>Context: New mobile app user: "Create a TikTok-style video feed" assistant: "I'll use the mobile-app-builder agent to build a performant feed with smooth scrolling." <commentary>Video feeds need careful optimization.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Mobile features user: "Add push notifications and biometrics" assistant: "I'll use the mobile-app-builder agent to add native push and Face ID/fingerprint auth." <commentary>Native features require platform-specific handling.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Cross-platform feature user: "We need this on iOS and Android" assistant: "I'll use React Native and the mobile-app-builder agent to keep native performance." <commentary>Cross-platform work must balance reuse and optimization.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: mobile-ux-optimizer
plugins.description: Use this agent to optimize UI/UX components for mobile‑first experiences, review design themes, and ensure mobile usability standards. Examples: <example>Context: Optimizing a desktop component for mobile user: 'I've built this navigation component but it’s not working well on mobile' assistant: 'I'll use the mobile‑ux‑optimizer agent to analyze and improve it for mobile‑first use.' <commentary>Use this agent for targeted mobile UX improvements.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Ensuring a new feature matches the design system user: 'I'm adding a new form component; ensure it matches our design system' assistant: 'I'll use the mobile‑ux‑optimizer agent to align it with your theme and mobile‑first principles.' <commentary>This agent ensures theme consistency and mobile optimization.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: model-context-protocol-mcp-expert
plugins.description: Model Context Protocol Mcp Expert subagent

plugins.name: monitoring-observability-specialist
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to implement comprehensive monitoring, observability, and alerting systems for enterprise B2B applications. This agent specializes in APM, logging, metrics, distributed tracing, SLA monitoring, and proactive incident management for business-critical systems. Examples:

plugins.name: n8n-workflow-builder
plugins.description: Use this agent to design, build, validate, or optimize n8n automation workflows. It specializes in efficient workflow design, validation, and MCP tool integration. Covers workflow creation (e.g., GitHub-to-Slack automations), troubleshooting failing workflows and debugging node errors, and recommending optimal nodes and workflow patterns for tasks like CSV processing and email reports.

plugins.name: onomastophes
plugins.description: Use proactively for generating creative non-olympian Greek god names with rich backstories, mythological authenticity, and modern accessibility for storytelling projects

plugins.name: performance-benchmarker
plugins.description:
Use this agent for comprehensive performance testing, profiling, and optimization recommendations. This agent specializes in measuring speed, identifying bottlenecks, and providing actionable optimization strategies for applications. Examples:

<example>
Context: Application speed testing

plugins.name: planning-prd-agent
plugins.description: 'MUST BE USED PROACTIVELY when user mentions: planning, PRD, product requirements document, project plan, roadmap, specification, requirements analysis, feature breakdown, technical spec, project estimation, milestone planning, or task decomposition. Use IMMEDIATELY when user says "create a PRD", "plan this feature", "document requirements", "break down this project", "estimate this work", "create a roadmap", "write specifications", or references planning/documentation needs. Expert Technical Project Manager that creates comprehensive PRDs with user stories, acceptance criteria, technical architecture, task breakdowns, and separate task assignment files for sub-agent delegation.'

plugins.name: prd-specialist
plugins.description: Use this agent to create comprehensive PRDs combining business strategy, technical architecture, and user research. Examples: <example>Context: Creating a PRD for a new feature user: "I need a PRD for our new authentication system with SSO and MFA" assistant: "I'll use the prd-specialist agent to create a PRD covering strategy, requirements, and implementation." </example> <commentary>Use this agent for full PRDs that guide development.</commentary> <example>Context: Major product initiative user: "We're launching a mobile app for our e‑commerce platform and need a detailed PRD" assistant: "I'll use the prd-specialist agent to produce a PRD with market analysis, user insights, technical architecture, and a roadmap." </example> <commentary>This agent ensures PRDs align strategy, research, and architecture.</commentary>

plugins.name: pricing-packaging-specialist
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to optimize B2B pricing strategies, packaging models, and revenue optimization for enterprise sales. This agent specializes in value-based pricing, usage-based billing, enterprise contract negotiations, and competitive pricing analysis for SaaS platforms. Examples:

plugins.name: problem-solver-specialist
plugins.description: Universal expert problem-solving agent specializing in complex debugging, mysterious runtime behavior, integration issues, and multi-layered technical challenges across any technology stack or project type. Uses advanced research methodologies including GitHub issues mining, Perplexity deep research, community solutions validation, browser automation testing, and multi-source documentation analysis. Proactively use for intricate bugs, cryptic error messages, performance anomalies, framework integration problems, legacy system compatibility issues, and any technical challenges requiring deep investigation across multiple knowledge sources. Project-agnostic and universally applicable.

plugins.name: product-sales-specialist
plugins.description: Use this agent when you need to support B2B sales through product design, user research, project management, and creative RFP responses. This agent specializes in creating compelling product demonstrations, design-focused RFP sections, user experience narratives, and project management frameworks that win enterprise deals. Combines product expertise with sales enablement. Examples:

plugins.name: project-curator
plugins.description: Reorganizes project structure by cleaning root clutter, creating logical folder hierarchies, and moving files to optimal locations. Tracks dependencies and fixes broken imports/paths. Use PROACTIVELY when project structure becomes unwieldy or needs architectural cleanup.

plugins.name: project-shipper
plugins.description: PROACTIVELY use this agent for launch milestones, release deadlines, and go-to-market work. It coordinates launches, manages release execution, and drives GTM strategy within the 6-day cycle. Handles major feature launch orchestration, coordinated multi-release sprint planning, go-to-market positioning and narrative crafting, and post-launch metric monitoring with rapid response coordination.

plugins.name: python-expert
plugins.description:
Use this agent for advanced Python work: performance tuning, profiling, and safe refactors (incl. async/concurrency and design patterns). Examples:

<example>Context: Slow function on large datasets. user: "This function is too slow"
assistant: "I'll use the python-expert agent to profile hotspots and optimize the code."</example>

<example>Context: Convert sync code to async/await. user: "Convert this to async/await"
assistant: "I'll use the python-expert agent to refactor with async/await and proper concurrency."</example>

<example>Context: Advanced patterns (decorators/factory). user: "Factory pattern with decorators for API endpoints"
assistant: "I'll use the python-expert agent to implement the pattern cleanly and idiomatically."</example>

plugins.name: rapid-prototyper
plugins.description: Use this agent to rapidly create app prototypes, MVPs, or proof-of-concepts within a 6-day cycle. It specializes in fast scaffolding, trending feature integration, and functional demos. Ideal for new app scaffolding and MVP builds, quick trend-testing prototypes (e.g., AI avatars), lightweight MVPs for business idea validation, and investor-ready functional demos that showcase the product vision.

plugins.name: react-native-dev
plugins.description:
Use this agent for React Native help: code review, component builds, debugging, performance tuning, and architecture. Examples:

<example>Context: Navigation issue. user: 'My stack navigator breaks between screens' assistant: 'Using react-native-dev to review your navigation setup and suggest a fix' <commentary>React Native navigation problem—use react-native-dev for targeted guidance.</commentary></example>

<example>Context: New component matching app patterns. user: 'Create a custom button component for our design system' assistant: 'Using react-native-dev to generate a button component aligned with your codebase and design patterns' <commentary>Follow existing React Native patterns—use react-native-dev for compatible component work.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: reddit-community-builder
plugins.description: Reddit Community Builder subagent

plugins.name: sprint-prioritizer
plugins.description: Use this agent when planning short development cycles, prioritizing features, managing roadmaps, or making trade‑off decisions. It focuses on maximizing value within tight timelines. Examples: <example>Context: Sprint planning user: "We have many requests but only 6 days" assistant: "I'll use the sprint‑prioritizer agent to create a focused, high‑impact sprint plan." <commentary>Sprint planning balances value, limits, and goals.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Feature trade‑offs user: "Build AI chat or improve onboarding?" assistant: "I'll evaluate ROI and impact using the sprint‑prioritizer agent to pick the best option." <commentary>Prioritization weighs effort, impact, and fit.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Mid‑sprint scope change user: "CEO wants video calling added" assistant: "I'll reassess priorities with the sprint‑prioritizer agent to protect sprint goals." <commentary>Scope changes need careful rebalancing.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: studio-coach
plugins.description: PROACTIVELY use this agent for complex multi‑agent work, when agents are stuck, overwhelmed, or need coordination. It acts as a performance coach to keep agents focused and composed, triggering automatically on challenging projects or signs of confusion. Examples: <example>Context: Starting a complex project user: "We need to build a viral TikTok app in 2 weeks" assistant: "Bringing in the studio‑coach to align and elevate the team." <commentary>Coaching maintains focus and motivation.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Agent stuck user: "I'm struggling to identify the right trends" assistant: "Activating the studio‑coach to refocus and strengthen your approach." <commentary>The coach restores clarity.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Before a sprint user: "Tomorrow we start a 6‑day sprint" assistant: "Calling in the studio‑coach to prepare the team for peak execution." <commentary>Pre‑sprint coaching boosts alignment.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: studio-producer
plugins.description: PROACTIVELY use this agent to coordinate teams, manage resources, and optimize studio workflows within 6-day cycles. It handles cross-functional alignment, capacity planning, and resolving workflow issues. Covers cross-team collaboration scheduling and handoff definition, resource allocation under constraints, QA and release workflow optimization, and sprint coordination including kickoff alignment and priority setting.

plugins.name: support-responder
plugins.description:
Use this agent when handling customer support inquiries, creating support documentation, setting up automated responses, or analyzing support patterns. This agent excels at maintaining high-quality support across all studio projects while identifying product improvement opportunities. Examples:

<example>
Context: Setting up support for a new app launch

plugins.name: web-dev

It supports code analysis, component creation, debugging, performance tuning, and architecture guidance. Examples: <example>Context: Next.js routing issue user: 'My dynamic routes in Next.js are not rendering correctly' assistant: 'I'll use the web-dev agent to review your Next.js routing setup and suggest a fix.' <commentary>Next.js-specific routing problems should use the web-dev agent for targeted guidance.</commentary></example> <example>Context: Reusable React component with Tailwind CSS user: 'I need a card component that matches my Tailwind design system' assistant: 'I'll use the web-dev agent to create a reusable TypeScript React card styled with Tailwind CSS.' <commentary>The web-dev agent ensures components align with your Tailwind-based design system.</commentary></example>

plugins.name: whimsy-injector
plugins.description: PROACTIVELY use this agent after any UI/UX change to add playful, delightful moments. It injects joy, surprise, and personality into experiences and should trigger after design updates. Covers enhancing new UI features like onboarding flows with delightful touches, making error and empty states friendly and low-friction, adding fun to standard components like loading spinners, and auditing finished features for surprise-and-delight opportunities.
